Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

What to do with incense packets with Krsnas picture on them

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-08-02 · 9 answers
Sometimes incense packets have Krsnas picture or Krsnas name on them so I feel bad about throwing them to the garbage. Is there some other option so I can stop collecting them?

I heard that burning is the last option...
user [78] · 2007-08-02
I always keep them in a drawer. I dont like to throw them away, it just doesnt seem right. So what if I have three drawers full with packets. To throw them away is not good...
user [2] · 2007-08-02
I collect all this kind of stuff, like damaged books, temple flyers, etc in one place and then burn them once in a while.
user [38] · 2007-08-02
Not too much damaged books can be donated to libraries in prisons, hospitals, etc. directly or thru Prison Ministry or Sastra Dana.
user [78] · 2007-08-02
but what can you do with the empty packets. It hurts burning them
user [24] · 2007-08-02
Burning them is a proper means of disposing of them.

Better is if you live by the Ganges and can put them in the current.

Burying them underground is also acceptable.

:)
user [72] · 2007-08-04
but if they are made of plastic and things like that its not so green to put them in the Ganges, no?
user [24] · 2007-08-04
i suppose putting plastic things in the Ganga isnt so great. Maybe its better to avoid buying the plastic ones then.
user [106] · 2007-08-05
I just put them aside somewhere and when I have time ill cut them to use them as bookmarkers.. or use them straight away...
user [168] · 2007-09-09
collect them together,tie them in a bag,bury or burn them,Praying to krishna to forgive you.
